title: IPONE Trail Ride
otherDetails:
We kindly ask you to arrive with a clean vehicle, bike, and footwear

The Ipone Trail Ride series is on this summer under the management of the Kaipara Motorcycle Club with 4 planned events over a number of different farms in the Kaipara Flats, Rodney area. Each track is marked out clearly for all levels of riders to enjoy.

There is food available at each event with sign-on starting at around 8.30am.

2026 Summer Trail Ride Series
Nov 30 Gaylene's Farm
Jan 18 Waynes Farm
Feb 15 Lances Farm
Mar 15 Scotts Farm

For further information on the 2025 season you can check us out on our Facebook page.

As these events are run on working farms, we cannot allow any dogs on site as they may interfere with livestock.

What your will need:

Helmets are compulsory
Boots (NO SNEAKERS OR GUMBOOTS)
Body Amour and Gloves recommended
A well prepared motorcycle makes the rides more enjoyable.

The purpose of the Kaipara Motorcycle Club is to provide premium off road motorcycle events in the Kaipara Flats, Rodney area and to especially promote and encourage junior and family riding. These events will be run to Motorcycling New Zealand Standard Operating Procedures, for the safety of all riders and event organisers.

title: Waikato Warrior Adventure Ride
otherDetails:
Info and Entry

Waikato Warrior is a GPS navigated adventure ride taking riders over 500km in a day, including loads of new offshoots onto private farmland and forestry roads and tracks in and around the Southern Waikato region starting and finishing in Te Awamutu.  We've altered the route from last year making it even better than before and will take us over some superb private tracks we havent had the privilege of exploring before! This is a fantastic ride, more like a trail ride with fantastic back- roads to link it all up!

The adventure will take you on a journey through some spectacular scenic areas with many awesome trails through private farms and forestry. Depending on the weather, there may be sections that will require a moderate level of off-road rider skills. These sections may include short steeper inclines and declines, slippery/muddy terrain or stream crossings. There will be shortcuts in place for those that want to finish a little earlier, as this is going to be 1 fantastic BIG day if you complete it all.

Note that it is important that you, or at least one rider in your group is competent at using GPS form of navigation.

Ride fee $150 includes:
 

  Friday night registration and briefing
  Sweep crew and support vehicle
  GPS co-ordinates
  All Land Access Fees
 

REGISTRATION MEET & GREET:

Registration Friday 20th March from 6.00pm with briefing 8.00pm. This will be at  at Redoubt Bar & Eatery, 423 Alexandra St, Te Awamutu. A great pre-ride chance for everyone to have a catch-up and a chinwag, and ensure you are all prepared to get away early the following morning without any hitches. This will include a pre-ride briefing, where all riders must sign-on, and attend for the briefing at 8pm.

Then it’s all go EARLY Saturday morning to head out and start your adventure. This is a BIG day's riding, so don't expect to be home early afternoon unless you take the shortcuts! We can meet back at the Bar late afternoon, early evening for some well deserved refreshments and to swap some stories!

Entries are limited so please register your interest on this page to be the first informed when entries are open!
originalAddress: 423 Alexandra Street, Te Awamutu 3800, New Zealand

title: Power Adventures Honda Kids Camp - Locinver Station
otherDetails:
Welcome to Lochinvar!

The largest Station in the North Island. This humongous property is our final stop for 2026 – a location we now get to visit every year!

These tracks are known for their elevation and grand rolling paddocks as far as the eye can see. This is also our most hospitable location with real flush toilets and showers, as we are able to utilise the shearing quarters and woolshed.

Speaking of…who’s got talent? Lochniver does!
You’ve probably heard of America’’s Got Talent, but you might not have heard about an even bigger show – Lochinver’s Got Talent! That is right. This camp show-cases the amazing talent of our campers in a not-to-be-missed, star-studded line up of singers, dancers and talented campers, who all compete for the coveted crown of Lochinvar’s Got Talent.

This is an event not to be missed.

And with the inclusion of the all new Disco Dance Party - this camp rules!!

Arrivals can be from 2pm Thursday afternoon (March 26th) to set up camp, ready for riding on Friday morning.

If you want to learn more about our Kids Camps – please visit www.poweradventures.co.nz

title: Korito Technical Matrix
otherDetails:
ONE DAY EVENT IN 2026

Supplementary Regs

come along and cheer Mitch on to do Gold again!
 

"Once a year the gates are opened up for the Korito Technical Matrix ( KTM ). The training tracks are joined up and you get to challenge yourself ( & your mates ) around a standard XC loop. Included in the lap are some 'single track' for the Silver and Gold classes. Plus at the end of each lap, is a matrix challenge. Great viewing beside the car park for spectators of the matrix.

Here is Patrick Dillon, showcasing the event from 2021:
 

Live streaming link for this event will be provided closer to the time. Also near real time scoring of qualification and main event scoring, has been considerably improved this year. Thanks to SilverBullet for investing in this one of a kind event in New Zealand.

Live scoring results will be shown on the big screen, weather permitting !

It's a quick fire fun event with a morning prologue plus an afternoon 2 hour XC race. There will be 'pay it forward spot prizes' for the best post-race stories, a bbq and lollies for young. (and not so young)

Prize vouchers are up for grabs across all Gold, Silver, Bronze

'Chicken lines' have been added, for all classes, to keep the afternoon race flowing.

7:45 - 8:45 Riders sign in
9:00 SHARP! Riders briefing
9:30 Bronze qualification
10:00 Silver qualification
10:30 Gold (and LCQ) qualifications
11:40 Main event briefing
12:15 race starts, where riders, will leave at 1 minute intervals
1500 (approx) Prize giving with coffee, ice cream, and lollies

Race will be 4 laps for each class ( Gold Silver Bronze ). Total race time around the two hour mark.

Each lap for Gold/Silver/Bronze includes riding the matrix section.  

Penalties per lap, will be a foot dab in the matrix,

1 = 10 secs
2 = 20 secs
3+ = 30 secs
5 ( unable to complete matrix as per normal sections rules ) = 50 secs penalty

The XC lap time plus the matrix scoring will accumulate to provide your lap time and class results. 

Winner of race ( per class ) will receive a bag of lollies. Spot prizes for everyone.

Back to the qualification, this result will *only* determine your start time of the main event. No penalties will be carried forward to the main race. Fastest qualification time, means you start first. Slowest means you start last.

Fastest qualifier ( of each class ) gets to choose whether to start 1,2,3 in their respective class.
eg. Fastest picks 3rd start place, 2nd fastest can choose 1 or 2 start place, 3rd fastest gets whatever is left from either 1,2,3

4th fastest starts 4th
5th fastest starts 5th
etc

Parking will be down by the old rotary cowshed, beside the matrix.

Kitchen, shower and toilet facilities are an easy walk up the hill to the new rotary cowshed.

BBQ supplied after the event, around 230pm. Depending on the weather; ice creams may be supplied too !

Sounds too good to be true - come along and find out.

See you there.

title: THE PINES - HARD ENDURO MARAMARUA
otherDetails:
Limited to 100 riders, so don't miss out.

Everyone who enters between February 1st and March 1st receives a free THE PINES - HARD ENDURO MARAMARUA T-Shirt.

Entries Open on February 1st and close April 13

 Sign-On from 8am. 
 Prolouge at 10am sharp
 Race Starts at 11:30am

Cost $100 (Late entries available on the day @ +$40)

Set deep in the Maramarua pine forest, THE PINES is the new hard enduro. long, technical, and relentlessly physical. 

In the same format as Erzberg, you have 3-hours to complete the course or you are classified as a DNF.

For the riders at the front, prize money awaits, but for everyone else, simply crossing the finish line will be the real victory.

Want to know what you're made of.....THE PINES is where you’ll find out.

title: Sand Country Run - Charity Trail Ride
otherDetails:
Charity Trail Ride

 
Sign in is from 8am , rider brief 9.30am, ride start 10am ,ride closes 3pm .

Cost per rider: Adults $50,

Children under 13 $15 dollars 

Family of 2 adults and 3 kids $110

CASH ONLY for entry fee 

lunch provided .

ENTRY CONDITIONS - PLEASE READ CAREFULLY:

Most rides suitable for two and four wheelers of all ability levels
All riders must wear helmet, gloves, long sleeves and sturdy long trousers (no shorts or t-shirts), same for kids track.
Riders of all bikes 65cc & over must wear motorcycle boots (no work boots etc).
Ride suitable for two, three and for wheel bikes with exception side by side  no side by sides.
All motorcycles must have adequate mufflers, functional brakes and good offroad tyres.
All riders must stay on the marked track and obey Marshals at all times.
No pillions.
Riding in reverse against the flow of the ride is strictly forbidden.
No dogs
You are REQUIRED to bring a toolkit/spares for YOUR bike.
Alcohol NOT permitted
Quads carry tow rope.
Please insure bikes are fit for ride it is a big loop ,two stroke bring spare plugs .
Rain hail sun shine this ride is going ahead .
By entering an event, you agree to comply with rules as listed here, given at rider briefing, or as set down by Motorcycling NZ.

All events run under permits issued by Motorcycling New Zealand.
